heading: Stop, Observe, Ask, and Respond to Health and Wellness Training Program
label: 300d–54 Stop, Observe, Ask, and Respond to Health and Wellness Training Program

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to be known as the Stop, Observe, Ask, and Respond to Health and Wellness Training Program or the SOAR to Health and Wellness Training Program (in this section referred to as the “Program”) to provide training to health care and social service providers on human trafficking in accordance with this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Program shall include the Stop, Observe, Ask, and Respond to Health and Wellness Training Program’s activities existing on the day before , and the authorized initiatives described in paragraph (2).December 31, 2018

children.label: i
children.text: for supporting activities under subsection (c); and

children.label: ii
children.text: that adapts to changing needs, settings, health care providers, and social service providers;

children.label: B
children.text: providing technical assistance to grantees related to implementing activities described in subsection (c) and reporting on any best practices identified by the grantees;

children.label: C
children.text: developing a reliable methodology for collecting data, and reporting such data, on the number of human trafficking victims identified and served by grantees in a manner that, at a minimum, prevents disclosure of individually identifiable information consistent with all applicable privacy laws and regulations; and

children.label: D
children.text: integrating, as appropriate, the training described in paragraphs (1) through (4) of subsection (c) with training programs, in effect on , for health care and social service providers for victims of intimate partner violence, sexual assault, stalking, child abuse, child neglect, child maltreatment, and child sexual exploitation.December 31, 2018
identify potential human trafficking victims;

implement best practices for working with law enforcement to report and facilitate communication with human trafficking victims, in accordance with all applicable Federal, State, local, and tribal laws, including legal confidentiality requirements for patients and health care and social service providers;
implement best practices for referring such victims to appropriate health care, social, or victims service agencies or organizations; and
provide such victims with coordinated, age-appropriate, culturally relevant, trauma-informed, patient-centered, and evidence-based care.
geography;
the demographics of the population to be served;
the predominant types of human trafficking cases involved; and
health care and social service provider profiles.

children.label: A
children.text: The total number of entities that received a grant under this section.

children.label: B
children.text: The total number and geographic distribution of health care and social service providers trained through the Program.

children.heading: Initial report
children.label: 2 Initial report
children.text: In addition to the data required to be collected under paragraph (1), for purposes of the initial report to be submitted under paragraph (3), the Secretary shall collect data on the total number of facilities and health care professional organizations that were operating under, and the total number of health care and social service providers trained through, the Stop, Observe, Ask, and Respond to Health and Wellness Training Program existing prior to the establishment of the Program under this section.

children.heading: Annual report
children.label: 3 Annual report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit an annual report to Congress on the data collected under this subsection in a manner that, at a minimum, prevents the disclosure of individually identifiable information consistent with all applicable privacy laws and regulations.December 31, 2018

children.heading: Sharing best practices
children.label: f Sharing best practices
children.text: The Secretary shall make available, on the Internet website of the Department of Health and Human Services, a description of the best practices and procedures used by entities that receive a grant for carrying out activities under this section.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: g Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “human trafficking” has the meaning given the term “severe forms of trafficking in persons” as defined in .section 7102 of title 22

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section, $4,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2020 through 2024.
